purpose: Define configurable project visit restrictions and the minimum data that must be visible in the postulation card before a shopper applies.
principle: Cinepolis franja rules are project-specific. Other projects may use age, visit frequency, last visit date, profile, time, day, location or client-specific restrictions.

cinepolisDefault.notes: For Cinepolis, project frequency is monthly and measurement/execution period is quincenal. This comes from client requirement and must remain configurable, not global.

postulationCardRules: The shopper must see honorarium, reimbursement, scenario, project summary and restrictions before applying.
If a visit has profile restrictions, the postulation card must show them clearly.
If a date proposal is allowed, the card must show availableFrom, allowed days/time/franja and measurement period.
If restrictions are client requirements, the card must label them as client requirement.
If restrictions are internal progress measurement, the card must label them as internal control or operational measurement.
If eligibility is uncertain, the card must route to manual review rather than silently allow/deny.
The card must not promise HR synchronization when HR sync gate is off.
overrideRules: Admin/ops may approve exceptions to profile, frequency or schedule restrictions only with authorizedBy and authorizedReason.
Exceptions must define whether they affect shopper score or not.
No automatic shopper penalty until scoring contract is approved.

newLessons: How to read a postulation card
Difference between client requirement and internal measurement
How visit frequency and last visit restrictions work
What to do when a shopper is not eligible

hardStops: Do not hard-code Cinepolis restrictions globally.
Do not hide restrictions from the shopper before postulation.
Do not automatically penalize shoppers until scoring contract is approved.
Do not write Firestore from this contract.
Do not show HR sync as active when gate is off.
